human_responses.response_text:
Pre-approval amendment — author-derived intent as a first-class mode. The change-author/session-derived intent path (-m) is currently framed in FR-2.1 only as a tolerated escape hatch from a circularity it warns against. Promote it to an explicitly supported intent-provenance spectrum, accepting the circularity as a deliberate ease-of-use trade:

1. Provenance spectrum (amend FR-2.1). Recognize three intent sources by independence: (a) tracker-independent — ticket authored independently of the fix (strongest); (b) ticket-seeded + session-refined — a vague ticket ('this didn't work right') expanded during the bug-fix session into a concrete statement (common, supported); (c) author session-summary — no ticket, statement synthesized from the working session (supported, weakest). Note that the review still validates implementation-correctness, stated-acceptance coverage, regressions, and quality even when the 'is this the right problem?' axis is non-independent.

2. Record provenance honestly (new FR / extend FR-2.4 + FR-8). intent.md and the run manifest must tag intent provenance and an independence flag (e.g. tracker | tracker+session | author-session-summary). The reviewer prompt is told the provenance so it calibrates how much weight to place on the problem-correctness axis (data over inference).

3. Human-in-the-loop review of synthesized intent (the mitigation). When intent is author/session-derived rather than a clean independent ticket, the synthesized problem statement must be surfaced to the human to review/edit before the review run starts. A human ratifying the problem statement is what makes the accepted circularity tolerable. Specify where this hook lives (CLI confirmation vs. the calling skill).

4. Scope decision — /gauntlet-review skill. A Claude Code skill that synthesizes intent from the active session and/or a vague linked ticket, presents it for in-session human review, invokes 'gauntlet review -m "<approved statement>"' with provenance, then hands off to /gauntlet-operator. Decide whether this skill is in-scope here or a follow-on; either way, ensure the CLI exposes what the skill needs (non-interactive -m plus a provenance argument).

steps.notes: converged in round 1 (blocking policy): no open blocking; 10 fixed, 0 non-blocking item(s) surfaced for the gate
awaiting human decision; review: prd.md, findings.json, triage.json, confirm.json
